note: All flight paths described from RHBH perspective. LHBH and forehand players should mirror left/right references.

stability_key.overstable: High fade rating. Resists turning in wind. Reliable and predictable in adverse conditions.
stability_key.stable: Neutral. Flies the way you throw it. Most predictable in calm conditions.
stability_key.understable: Turns right before fading left (RHBH). Great for distance and flex shots but risky in headwinds.

angle_key.hyzer: Throwing-side edge lower at release. Promotes left finish (RHBH). More wind-resistant.
angle_key.flat: Level at release. Neutral flight path.
angle_key.anhyzer: Throwing-side edge higher at release. Promotes right turn. Risky in headwinds.

disc_categories.driver: Max distance. Needs higher arm speed to fly correctly. Most affected by wind.
disc_categories.mid: Moderate distance. Easier to control. Good for accuracy shots.
disc_categories.putter: Shortest range. Most accurate. Excellent for short shots and putting.

shot_shapes.straight: Disc travels directly toward target with minimal left/right deviation.
shot_shapes.short_left: Disc finishes left of the line within short-to-mid range.
shot_shapes.short_right: Disc finishes right of the line within short-to-mid range.
shot_shapes.long_left: Disc carries full distance and finishes far left.
shot_shapes.long_right: Disc carries full distance and finishes far right.
shot_shapes.s_curve: Disc turns right early in flight then fades left at the end.
